# Deployment Summary — Namespace: `demo-diskpressure`

## Overview
Four Deployments found, all targeting a disk-pressure scenario node. Three are healthy; **one is critically degraded**.

---

## Deployment Status at a Glance

| Deployment | Replicas | Status | Role |
|---|---|---|---|
| `log-collector` | 1/1 Ready | ✅ Available | noise |
| `nginx-cache` | 1/1 Ready | ✅ Available | noise |
| `postgres-emptydir` | 1/1 Ready | ✅ Available | managed |
| `redis-scratch` | 0/1 Ready | 🔴 **DEGRADED** | noise |

---

## 🔴 Critical: `redis-scratch` — ProgressDeadlineExceeded

**This deployment is actively failing and is the primary incident signal.**

- **Status:** `Available=False`, `Progressing=False`
- **Failure reason:** `ProgressDeadlineExceeded` — ReplicaSet `redis-scratch-f9d747f65` timed out progressing (deadline: 600s)
- **Replicas:** 2 total, **2 unavailable**, 0 ready — indicates a stuck rollout with old and new pods both failing
- **Last status update:** `2026-05-04T14:37:03Z`
- **Containers:**
  - `redis` (`redis:7-alpine`): AOF persistence enabled (`--appendonly yes`), writes to `/data` (emptyDir, **150Mi limit**)
  - `session-loader` (busybox): Writes 200 × 512K files = **~100MB** of random data to `/data` over ~400s, then idles
- **Volume:** `emptyDir` with `sizeLimit: 150Mi` — the session-loader writes ~100MB of AOF segments directly into the same volume Redis uses for persistence. Combined with Redis's own AOF data, this is likely **breaching the 150Mi emptyDir cap**, causing pod eviction/failure and contributing to node disk pressure.

---

## Disk-Pressure Contributing Deployments (All Healthy but Intentionally Writing)

### `log-collector` (✅ Running)
- **Container:** `logger` (busybox)
- **Behavior:** Writes a new 32K random log file every 60 seconds to `/var/log/app`
- **Volume:** `emptyDir`, `sizeLimit: 50Mi`
- **Risk:** Low individual impact; continuous file accumulation

### `nginx-cache` (✅ Running)
- **Init container:** `cache-warmup` — writes **200 × 1MB = ~200MB** of random data to `/tmp/nginx-cache` over ~200s
- **Volume:** `emptyDir`, `sizeLimit: 250Mi` — init container attempts to write up to 200MB against a 250Mi cap; close to the limit
- **Main container:** `nginx` (`nginxinc/nginx-unprivileged:1.27-alpine`) — mounts same cache volume
- **Risk:** High one-time disk spike during init; currently stable post-warmup

### `postgres-emptydir` (✅ Running)
- **Container:** `postgres` (`quay.io/sclorg/postgresql-16-c9s`)
- **Volume:** `emptyDir`, **`sizeLimit: 8Gi`** — largest emptyDir in the namespace; primary disk pressure risk
- **Config:** PGDATA at `/var/lib/pgsql/data/userdata`; password from Secret `postgres-credentials`; init SQL from ConfigMap `postgres-init-sql`
- **Resources:** Limits 500m CPU / 2Gi memory
- **Risk:** 8Gi emptyDir on a disk-pressured node is the **largest single disk consumer** in the namespace

---

## Common Configuration (All Deployments)
- **Node selector & tolerations:** All target a specific scenario node with `NoSchedule` taints (scenario-specific disk pressure node) and tolerate `node.kubernetes.io/disk-pressure` (redacted but implied by taint key pattern)
- **Managed by:** ArgoCD (`argocd-controller`) + `kubectl` Apply
- **Strategy:** RollingUpdate (25% maxSurge/maxUnavailable)

---

## Incident Investigation Summary

| Finding | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Immediate failure** | `redis-scratch` stuck rollout — 2 unavailable replicas, ProgressDeadlineExceeded at 14:37 |
| **Root cause candidate** | `redis-scratch` emptyDir (150Mi) likely exhausted by session-loader writing ~100MB + Redis AOF data |
| **Largest disk consumer** | `postgres-emptydir` — 8Gi emptyDir on a disk-pressured node |
| **Sustained disk writer** | `log-collector` — continuous 32K writes every 60s |
| **One-time large write** | `nginx-cache` init — up to 200MB written during pod startup |
| **Node condition** | All pods tolerate `disk-pressure` taint, confirming node is already under disk pressure |

**Recommended next steps:** Check node disk usage and emptyDir consumption (`kubectl describe node`), inspect `redis-scratch` pod events for eviction messages, and evaluate whether the 8Gi postgres emptyDir or nginx cache warmup triggered the initial disk pressure condition.

kaResponse.analysis: Node dev-worker-1.redhat-internal.com is experiencing recurring DiskPressure on its /var/lib/kubelet loop device (10.46 GB ext4) due to cumulative emptyDir writes from workloads in the demo-diskpressure namespace. The postgres-emptydir Deployment is the dominant disk consumer with an 8Gi emptyDir volume — nearly exhausting the entire 10.46 GB loop device on its own. A sawtooth fill-evict-recover cycle repeats every ~20-30 minutes. Migrating postgres-emptydir from emptyDir to a PersistentVolumeClaim (backed by TopoLVM/LVMS already present on the cluster) will permanently eliminate the primary pressure source.

rootCauseAnalysis.summary: Node dev-worker-1.redhat-internal.com is experiencing recurring DiskPressure on its /var/lib/kubelet loop device (10.46 GB ext4) due to cumulative emptyDir writes from workloads in the demo-diskpressure namespace. The postgres-emptydir Deployment is the dominant disk consumer with an 8Gi emptyDir volume — nearly exhausting the entire 10.46 GB loop device on its own. A sawtooth fill-evict-recover cycle repeats every ~20-30 minutes. Migrating postgres-emptydir from emptyDir to a PersistentVolumeClaim (backed by TopoLVM/LVMS already present on the cluster) will permanently eliminate the primary pressure source.
causalChain: Signal: DiskPressure predicted (and already firing) on node dev-worker-1.redhat-internal.com
Why? The /var/lib/kubelet loop device (/dev/loop1, 10.46 GB ext4) is repeatedly filling to ~90% capacity, triggering kubelet ephemeral-storage eviction threshold

Why? Multiple workloads in demo-diskpressure namespace are simultaneously writing large volumes of data to emptyDir volumes backed by this loop device, with aggregate declared sizeLimit (~8.96 GiB) nearly equal to total device capacity

Why? The postgres-emptydir Deployment declares an 8Gi emptyDir for PostgreSQL data on a node whose kubelet loop device is only 10.46 GB — leaving <1.5 GB for all other pods, OS overhead, and kubelet metadata

Root cause: The demo-diskpressure namespace workloads were designed to generate disk pressure (intentional scenario), but the postgres-emptydir Deployment's 8Gi emptyDir is disproportionately large for the available loop device capacity, and combined with continuously writing sidecars in redis-scratch pods, the aggregate emptyDir usage exhausts the 10.46 GB kubelet filesystem in a repeating cycle

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: Targeting postgres-emptydir Deployment because its 8Gi emptyDir sizeLimit is the single largest disk consumer and the most actionable fix: reducing sizeLimit or migrating to a PVC would free the most space. The Node is the signal reporter but not the remediable resource — the fix must be applied to the workload configuration.
dueDiligence.proportionality: Remediation targets postgres-emptydir as the largest single contributor (8Gi of 10.46 GB device). Secondary contributors (redis-scratch session-loaders, nginx-cache init, log-collector) are listed in contributing_factors. A targeted fix to postgres-emptydir's storage configuration addresses the dominant cause without requiring changes to all four Deployments simultaneously.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: node conditions, node events, all pods in demo-diskpressure namespace, all Deployments, Prometheus metrics for both /var/lib/kubelet and /var filesystem, container fs usage metrics, pod logs (current and previous). Did not investigate: other namespaces' pods on the same node (possible additional disk consumers), actual kubelet image GC logs, or journald/OS log sizes. The demo-diskpressure namespace is the primary scope per the signal.
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced fully: DiskPressure signal → /var/lib/kubelet loop device filling → aggregate emptyDir writes from 4 co-located Deployments → postgres-emptydir 8Gi emptyDir as dominant consumer on 10.46 GB device. Cannot go deeper without inspecting actual PostgreSQL data volume growth rate, but the structural mismatch (8Gi declared on 10.46 GB device) is sufficient to explain the pressure.
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: All key claims are backed by tool evidence: node conditions from kubectl_describe, DiskPressure events from kubectl_events (EvictionThresholdMet at 13:20:21 UTC, NodeHasDiskPressure at 13:20:28 UTC), Prometheus range data showing sawtooth pattern on /var/lib/kubelet, pod inventory from kubectl_get_by_kind_in_namespace, Deployment specs from kubectl_describe. The only unverified claim is the exact rate of PostgreSQL data growth (logs show successful init but no ongoing write rate).
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: No prior remediation history available for either the Node or postgres-emptydir Deployment (both tier1 and tier2 history are empty). N/A — no previously failed remediations to avoid.
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: Considered: (1) /var (XFS, /dev/vda4) as the pressure source — ruled out: 63.8 GB total with ~16-17 GB consistently free, no pressure events on that filesystem. (2) Container image layer accumulation — possible contributor but node events specifically cite ephemeral-storage eviction, not image GC. (3) Single workload (redis-scratch) as sole cause — ruled out: redis-scratch emptyDir limits (150Mi/512Mi) are too small to alone exhaust 10.46 GB; postgres-emptydir's 8Gi is the dominant factor. (4) External disk writes (OS logs, journal) — possible minor contributor but not sufficient to explain GB-scale swings.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: 0.88: Starting at 1.0, reduced by: -0.05 for not verifying PostgreSQL actual data growth rate (only structural analysis); -0.04 for not checking other namespaces' pods on the same node that could contribute additional disk pressure; -0.03 for Prometheus node_filesystem_avail_bytes queries with instance label returning empty (had to use job=node-exporter filter), suggesting possible metric label variance that could affect trend accuracy.

contributingFactors: postgres-emptydir Deployment uses an 8Gi emptyDir for PostgreSQL data — the single largest disk consumer on a 10.46 GB kubelet loop device
redis-scratch session-loader sidecars continuously write ~100MB each to emptyDir volumes even while the redis container is stuck in ImagePullBackOff
nginx-cache init container writes ~200MB to a 250Mi emptyDir on every pod startup
log-collector continuously writes 32K files every 60 seconds to a 50Mi emptyDir
All workloads are co-located on dev-worker-1.redhat-internal.com via nodeSelector, concentrating all emptyDir pressure on a single loop device
Aggregate declared sizeLimit (~8.96 GiB) nearly equals total loop device capacity (10.46 GB), leaving virtually no headroom
DiskPressure already fired at 13:20:28 UTC with EvictionThresholdMet events; sawtooth pattern repeating every ~20-30 minutes
NoSchedule taint applied at 14:26:40 UTC does not address the root write pattern
